discussion_comments.comment_body: we should only write to the PTY here if there's an actual long running command
how much testing did u do on the interval/chunk size here? Its not a huge deal but given our bootstrap script is ~70kb this probably means it takes about a second to feed everything in?

We tested progressively larger single writes through `podman exec -it` (1KB, 2KB, 4KB, 8KB, 16KB, 32KB) and found the PTY buffer threshold is between 8–16KB — everything up to 8KB succeeded, 16KB+ failed. The minified bootstrap is ~32KB (comments are stripped), so 4KB chunks give solid margin below the threshold. The 50ms delay was a conservative choice — we didn't sweep different delay values, so it could likely be reduced if the ~400ms total overhead (8 chunks × 50ms) is a concern. Either way it only applies to docker/podman subshells, so normal sessions are unaffected.

This seems pretty inefficient-  it doesn't seem right to trigger render for every AI block based on status updates at the task level. Can we either unsubscribe after a certain point or listen to a more granular event
yeah good point — I think unsubscribing makes sense
`MAX_IMAGE_SIZE_BYTES_FOR_CLI_AGENT = 500MB` in `util/image.rs` and stat-first to reject without loading; ephemeral error toast names the file.

We should use only a small subset of bytes here rather than entire file - see example below

Should be able to reuse this kind of logic with `MIME_SNIFF_BYTES`

```
        let (file_size, mime_sniff_bytes) = file_size_and_prefix_for_path(&path, MIME_SNIFF_BYTES)?;

        Ok(Self {
            filepath: normalize_artifact_filepath(&path),

            path,
        })
```
Addressed in `9d9be00`. Now slicing to `MIME_SNIFF_BYTES` before calling `infer_mime_type`, and moved the constant from `artifact_upload.rs` to `util/image.rs` so both call sites share it.

Fixed in b1f7e7e. Added two guards: (1) skip entity diff computation for files already classified as `DiffSize::Large` or `DiffSize::Unrenderable`, and (2) cap the current file content at 4.375 MB (same threshold as the existing `MAX_DIFF_SIZE` in `diff_size_limits.rs`) before passing to `compute_entity_diff` — if exceeded, return `None`. This mirrors the existing safety boundary for line-based diffs.

discussion_comments.comment_body: Fixed in d6d3832: removed the `CapActiveBlockHeight` cap from `update_live_block_height()` entirely. The `CompactProgressLines` cache (which skips the SumTree rebuild when the height hasn't changed between frames) is sufficient to solve the performance problem without breaking scroll math. Kept the flag as a placeholder for future work that could apply the cap at the rendering/viewport layer instead of the stored height.
Why do we need to wrap this in Arc now?

The main change is in https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/pull/10314/changes#diff-2fcff7c4f4f049e80ea75516a1e9b2a59974bc492260ebf01661419d372eb022R493-R503

On master, the `Arc::try_unwrap` failure triggered a full reload but I've changed it to instead borrow + clone the value and since `FileDiffAndContent` is an expensive clone, I wrapped it with an `Arc`
